title: Wire tier_fallbacks as a within-tier MODEL-failover axis (Fable 5 → Opus → Sonnet on exit-85) — the missing fallback dimension that hard-blocks the audit phase
root_cause: The fallback policy has 3 axes: CLI (cli_fallback), tier (TierChain/DispatchTiered #331), and MODEL-within-a-tier. The audit phase disables the first two BY DESIGN — auditor.json has model_tier_envelope {min:deep,max:deep} (tier pinned, TierChain(deep,deep)=[deep], step-down is a no-op) and cli_fallback:[] (empty to preserve builder!=auditor adversarial invariant: codex=builder, agy banned). So audit resolves to a SINGLE point (claude-tmux, deep) → the one model in the catalog. The THIRD axis (next model at the same cli+tier) does not exist in code. The operator ALREADY authored the chain: .evolve/model-catalog.json claude.tier_fallbacks.deep = [fable, opus, sonnet]. But modelcatalog/catalog.go modelForTier() collapses a tier to a SINGLE model — it returns tier_models[tier] (='fable') immediately and only reads tier_fallbacks[tier] when tier_models[tier] is EMPTY, and even then returns only the FIRST element. Repo-wide grep confirms nothing consumes tier_fallbacks at dispatch time (only cmd_models/refresh MergeFallbacks preserve it across catalog rebuilds). Result: when claude deep (=Fable 5) hits its per-model usage limit ('You've reached your Fable 5 limit … /usage-credits' — captured verbatim in .evolve/runs/cycle-913/escalation-report.json), the dispatch has NO alternative model even though Opus (also deep, same CLI) has budget → ErrAllFamiliesExhausted → cycle checkpoints, audit never completes. Cycles 908-915 (session 2026-07-18) all blocked here; 0 ships.
evidence: .evolve/profiles/auditor.json (model_tier_envelope min=max=deep; cli_fallback=[]); .evolve/model-catalog.json (claude tier_models.deep + tier_fallbacks.deep=[fable,opus,sonnet]); go/internal/modelcatalog/catalog.go:62 modelForTier (collapses to single model; tier_fallbacks only read when tier_models empty, returns first only); go/internal/bridge/catalog_overlay.go:94 (DispatchModel single-model overlay into ModelTierMap); go/internal/bridge/realizer.go:179,191 (tier→single model; a CONCRETE model value passes through unchanged — so a concrete model CAN be dispatched); go/internal/core/cyclerun_dispatch.go:213 (all-families-exhausted checkpoint); go/internal/llmroute/tier_fallback.go:68 DispatchTiered (walks tiers×candidates; NO model axis); grep: neither llmroute nor phases/runner reads the catalog (bridge-only). Live repro: .evolve/runs/cycle-913/escalation-report.json pane_tail shows the real Fable-5 wall.
fix: Add the within-tier MODEL-failover axis (innermost: model ⊂ tier ⊂ CLI), keyed on the operator's catalog chain. (1) modelcatalog: add Catalog.DispatchModelChain(cli, tier) []string — live-gated, returns tier_models[tier] first then tier_fallbacks[tier], deduped, order-preserving (the ordered concrete-model preference for the tier). (2) Injection point — CHOOSE (design review): (A) BRIDGE-LOCAL (preferred, preserves 'no runner-side model resolution'): bridge Engine.Launch resolves the model chain for (cli, BridgeRequest.Model=tier) and, on a driver exit-85 with models remaining, re-realizes with the next concrete model + re-dispatches, returning exit-85 only when the chain is exhausted. Model-knowledge stays in the bridge where realization already lives. (B) LLMROUTE-EXPANSION: thread the catalog into llmroute.Resolve so plan.Tiers expands the primary tier into its concrete-model chain (fable,opus,sonnet dispatched as concrete BridgeRequest.Model — realizer passes through); DispatchTiered then walks it for free. Simpler control-flow but leaks catalog into routing + muddies tier vs model in plan.Tiers. Recommend (A). (3) Respect the operator chain as authority (they put sonnet in deep's chain intentionally); no separate envelope re-filter needed at the model level. (4) Wire it so the immediate operator unblock (tier_models.deep=opus, done 2026-07-18) can be REVERTED to tier_models.deep=fable and the chain provides fable→opus automatically.
acceptance: Catalog.DispatchModelChain(claude, deep) == [fable, opus, sonnet] (live-sourced); non-live entry → nil (manifest fallback). Unit tested incl. dedupe + tier_models-first order.

Repro test (RED first): a driver that returns exit-85 for the FIRST model of a tier and 0 for the SECOND, dispatched at deep, results in a SUCCESSFUL phase via the second model (opus) — NOT ErrAllFamiliesExhausted. Proves the model axis engages before CLI/tier escalation.
Exhaustion terminal only when the WHOLE model chain walls (all of [fable,opus,sonnet] exit-85) — then ErrAllFamiliesExhausted as today.
auditor (cli_fallback=[], envelope deep-pinned) is UNBLOCKED when fable is walled but opus has budget — the exact 908-915 failure.
cd go && go test -race on touched pkgs + acs-durable green; go-reviewer (hot-path dispatch → adversarial) + code-simplifier; graduate any new internal pkg into .apicover-enforce (completeness gate).
source: operator-injection 2026-07-18: user root-caused why the loop cannot complete despite the fallback policy — audit is a single (claude,deep,fable) point; the model-within-tier axis is unwired though the catalog chain (fable,opus,sonnet) is authored. User directive: 'deep level model could use opus as fable's fallback option.' Immediate unblock (catalog deep→opus) applied live; this is the durable wiring.
